checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly translates the slide-based layout of the source PDF into a single-column, scrolling document format. Information is organized logically into sections that correspond to the original slides. Headers and bulleted lists are preserved in their respective sections. The transition from a fixed slide format to a responsive HTML layout is executed without loss of visual organization or intent.

The tables present on pages 6 and 8 of the source PDF are correctly rendered in the HTML. The 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' table on page 6 maintains its column structure and readability. The complex table at the bottom of page 8, which is split into two halves in the PDF, is faithfully reproduced in the HTML with matching headers and data alignment, allowing for clear understanding of the data.

All meaningful images, including maps, charts, and diagrams from the source PDF, are present in the HTML output. This includes the major maps on pages 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, and 14. Logos and banners (U.S. Army, Building Strong) are also consistently rendered where they appeared in the original slides.

There is no evidence of visual content duplication in the body of the HTML document. While headers appear in both the Table of Contents and the main body, this is a standard accessibility feature and not a defect. Repeating decorative elements like the organizational banners reflect their repeated presence across multiple slides in the source PDF.
